title: Reebelo Australia Pty Ltd — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: Reebelo Australia, an online marketplace for new and refurbished electronics, paid $59,400 in penalties after the ACCC issued three infringement notices alleging false or misleading statements about consumer guarantee rights. The statements purported to impose a 14-day time limit on consumers' ability to obtain a remedy for faulty, damaged, misdescribed or wrong-model goods, when the Australian Consumer Law entitles consumers to a remedy regardless of elapsed time. The ACCC noted particular concern given the products involved are often refurbished high-end electronics such as laptops and mobile phones.
scope: Paid $59,400 in penalties across three ACCC infringement notices for alleged false or misleading consumer-guarantee representations; Alleged representations imposed a 14-day limit on remedy rights that the Australian Consumer Law does not itself impose; ACCC identified potential financial harm given the higher-value, refurbished electronics products involved
